_comment: Schema definition — this array will be populated by red_team.py

_schema.id: string  — UUID v4 unique to this finding
_schema.type: string  — technique used: sqli | path_traversal | file_upload | brute_force | ssh_brute | mysql_anon | curl_probe
_schema.success: boolean — true if exploitation produced evidence of compromise
_schema.technique: string  — same as type, explicit tool used

evidence.raw_snippet: string  — first 2000 chars of tool output
evidence.dumped_tables: string[] — table names extracted by sqlmap
evidence.credentials: string[] — cracked credential lines from hydra
evidence.sql_error: boolean — true if a SQL error was triggered

_schema.raw_output: string  — full stdout+stderr from the exploit tool (capped 5000 chars)
_schema.sandbox_id: string  — Daytona sandbox UUID that produced this finding
_schema.depth: integer — recursion depth (0 = first wave, max MAX_DEPTH)
_schema.deeper_vectors: object[] — additional vectors spawned from this finding
_schema.error: string | null — non-null if the sandbox command failed
